_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Kildare, the 2025 Tailteann Cup winners (which earned automatic Sam Maguire qualification), beat Laois in the Leinster quarter-final and pushed Westmeath to extra time in the semi-final before losing; in the Sam Maguire they lost Round 1 to Galway and Round 2B to Kerry. Four championship games, just one win all season.
_meta.verification: All scorelines, venues and referees verified against primary tables and reports (RTE, gaa.ie, HoganStand, Munster GAA, Irish Examiner). Full scorer list held for the Westmeath semi-final; partial for the others (scorelines verified).

manager.name: Brian Flanagan
manager.note: Oversaw a disappointing season in which Kildare's only two wins (across league and championship) came against Laois and Offaly, and the county was relegated from Division 2. Their Sam Maguire place was inherited from the 2025 Tailteann Cup title.

source.snippet: Brian Flanagan and his management team have named the Kildare Senior Football Team to face Kerry... Kildare's only wins this year came against Offaly (Division 2) and Laois in the Leinster quarter-final.

league.note: A poor league campaign ended in relegation from Division 2 to Division 3 (alongside Offaly). Midfielder Kevin Feely had publicly backed the side to play a role in the championship despite the league form.

source.snippet: Both Kildare and Offaly were relegated to Division 3.
All-Ireland SFC Round 2B ... Kildare 0-17 Kerry 3-22, Cedral St Conleth's Park - FT
Quarter-Final Kildare 2-20 2-15 Laois Cedral St Conleth's Park

results.note: Kildare's only championship win of 2026, a five-point quarter-final defeat of Laois to reach the Leinster semi-final. Full scorer list not captured; to be filled on a verification pass.

scorers.note: all from play, in fine form
1 two-point free; last-second equaliser to force extra time

source.snippet: Scorers for Kildare: Eoin Cully 0-4, Darragh Kirwan 0-4, Ben Loakman 0-3, Alex Beirne 0-3 (1tpf), Darragh Swords 0-2 (tp), Jack Robinson 0-2, Tommy Gill 0-1, Kevin Feely 0-1, Callum Bolton 0-1, Brian McLoughlin 0-1f, Colm Moran 0-1.

results.note: Kildare wasted a strong position and were ultimately overrun in extra time. Alex Beirne's equaliser with ten seconds left forced the extra period, but a thin Westmeath side found more, Brandon Kelly's goal the difference. Reports noted Kildare's inaccuracy and their failure to finish off a 'punch-drunk' Westmeath. Full scorer list. Both sides hit two two-pointers. Westmeath's perspective is held in Westmeath's file.

source.snippet: All-Ireland SFC Round 1 ... Galway 3-21 Kildare 0-17, Pearse Stadium - FT

results.note: A 13-point opening loss away to a Galway side that started its All-Ireland campaign in style (Rob Finnerty prominent). The defeat dropped Kildare into the Round 2B knockout. Full Kildare scorer list not captured; Galway's file carries the opposite perspective. To be filled on a verification pass.

source.snippet: All-Ireland SFC Round 2B ... Kildare 0-17 Kerry 3-22, Cedral St Conleth's Park - FT

results.note: Elimination game. Kildare stayed in touch for long spells but Kerry pulled clear with three goals in the closing quarter for a 14-point win in front of almost 10,000 in Newbridge. Full Kildare scorer list not captured; Kerry's perspective will live in Kerry's file (Kerry still alive, semi-finalist). Referee: Niall Cullen. To be filled on a verification pass.

collection_meta.collected_by: Claude (research session, not yet hand-verified by Rory)
fields_not_found: Full Kildare scorer lists for the Laois Leinster QF, the Galway R1 loss and the Kerry R2B loss (scorelines verified; only the Westmeath semi-final is itemised)
collection_meta.notes: Kildare is a mid-to-light Leinster county with a deflating arc: a Sam Maguire place earned by the previous year's Tailteann win, one championship win all season (Laois), a near-miss against Westmeath in the Leinster semi (let a strong position slip in extra time), then two heavy Sam Maguire defeats to Galway and Kerry. Division 2 relegation completed a poor year. Captain Kevin Feely, Darragh Kirwan, Eoin Cully, Ben Loakman, Alex Beirne and Darragh Swords are the names. The Westmeath semi-final is the one game with full scorer detail and the only competitive bright spot; a verification pass should fill the other three scorer lists from primary reports.
